commands: npm run tauri build immediately after normal close
proved: Every tracked text or vision engine stop uses one bounded reaper that inspects the child, requests termination, polls for an observed exit, and retains the managed handle if verification fails.
The shutdown command returns a typed report only after both tracked processes are reaped and local port 11435 no longer accepts connections.
Normal window close runs the same verification synchronously before destruction; a failure prevents close, logs the concrete error, and displays a native error dialog.
The updater awaits verified shutdown and no longer swallows shutdown errors or relies on a fixed delay before overwriting files.
Turning local AI off persists the off state only after verified shutdown succeeds.
Model replacement, vision-mode swaps, and vision-model deletion use the same reaper instead of kill-and-drop calls.
The Windows unit fixture held a DLL-shaped file with incompatible sharing, proved it was locked, and required immediate replacement after the production reaper returned.

The packaged app started its own llama-server, denied build-style exclusive read/write access to ggml-base.dll while running, accepted a normal close request, removed both processes, and allowed that access immediately afterward.
A complete installer build succeeded immediately after the packaged normal-close proof.
notProved: Cleanup after abrupt power loss, operating-system forced termination, kernel failure, or a process that Syzygy did not launch and does not own.
GPU-driver memory accounting reaches zero by a specific deadline; observed process exit is the operating-system resource-release boundary used here.
The native failure dialog was forced through its rare error branch in the packaged GUI.
Equivalent live packaged behavior on macOS or Linux; the process-reaping unit test is cross-platform, while the file-lock and packaged probes in this run are Windows-specific.
